articles.title: Israel And Lebanon Agree To Implement Ceasefire, Trump Admin Says
articles.content:
迪拜6月3日（路透社）——特朗普政府周三表示，以色列和黎巴嫩同意实施停火协议以结束敌对行动，这为结束美国-以色列对伊朗战争的更广泛协议带来了希望。

德黑兰此前曾将与美国达成协议的部分条件设定为结束以色列与黎巴嫩之间的战斗。伊朗早些时候袭击了科威特，破坏了其机场并造成数十人受伤，而美军则在霍尔木兹海峡附近进行了打击。

美国国务院在华盛顿谈判后发布的一份联合声明称，以色列-黎巴嫩停火取决于伊朗盟友真主党民兵完全停止开火，以及其所有人员从利塔尼河南部地区撤离。

双方上个月已同意停火，但敌对行动仍在继续。以色列于3月入侵黎巴嫩，追击黎巴嫩激进组织真主党，该组织向边境开火以支持德黑兰。

科威特当局和官方媒体称，伊朗无人机和导弹袭击损坏了机场设施和外交使团，造成一人死亡、60多人受伤，科威特国际机场航班暂停。

民航局表示，科威特航空和半岛航空在采取安全措施后恢复了航班。

据伊朗官方媒体报道，伊朗精锐革命卫队表示，他们没有向科威特机场开火，并将破坏归咎于未能击中目标的美军拦截导弹。

美军表示这一说法不准确，并称伊朗无人机故意以机场为目标。

早些时候，伊朗媒体报道革命卫队袭击了美国第五舰队在巴林的总部、一个美国空军基地，以及一艘被确认为"Panaya"号的船只。美国中央司令部否认其基地遭到袭击，并表示伊朗弹道导弹未能击中该地区的目标。

中央司令部表示，它在伊朗南部进行了新一轮"防御性打击"，目标是导弹发射场和试图布雷的伊朗船只，并在伊朗发动袭击后对霍尔木兹海峡附近的格什姆岛进行了打击。

自2月28日美国和以色列对伊朗发动打击以来，德黑兰反复袭击海湾地区的目标，那里是美国军事基地的所在地。

尽管4月初达成了停火协议，但近几周敌对行动仍不时升级。美国一直推动重新开放霍尔木兹海峡，该海峡在战前承担了全球约五分之一的石油和液化天然气运输。

上周，伊朗和美国表示在初步协议方面取得了进展，以停止战争并重新开放海峡，但双方尚未签署该协议，更复杂的谈判将留待以后进行。

伊朗外交部长阿巴斯·阿拉奇周三对黎巴嫩广播公司Al Mayadeen表示，谈判没有中断，但没有取得进展。

除将结束黎巴嫩战斗作为与美国的协议条件外，伊朗还要求获得数十亿美元石油收入的获取权、原油出口制裁豁免、解除对其港口的美国封锁，以及继续控制海峡。

面临压低燃油价格压力的美国总统特朗普表示，他的首要任务是阻止伊朗获得核武器。伊朗称其核计划用于和平目的。在周三发布的播客采访中，特朗普表示伊朗已同意不拥有核武器，哈梅内伊参与了谈判。

周三晚些时候，特朗普暗示与伊朗的谈判最快可能在本周末取得进展。

"如果发生的话，可能在本周末发生，"特朗普在白宫椭圆形办公室对记者说，但没有详细说明他预期在该时间范围内会发生什么。

特朗普表示，各方正在努力将重新开放海峡的问题与黎巴嫩的冲突分开。

以色列继续对黎巴嫩发动打击

这场战争造成数千人死亡，主要在伊朗和黎巴嫩，同时通过严重扰乱能源供应和其他航运造成全球经济痛苦。

这也引发了以色列与真主党之间最新一轮冲突。

黎巴嫩安全消息人士称，周三，以色列无人机袭击在黎巴嫩南部造成至少六人死亡，并瞄准了贝鲁特以南的一辆汽车，而以色列表示拦截了一架可能是真主党发射的可疑飞机。

阿拉奇表示，如果以色列攻击贝鲁特，伊朗将做出果断回应。

在播客讲话中，特朗普承认在一次据报道充满粗话的通话中曾称以色列总理内塔尼亚胡"疯了"，当时他正在就更大范围的战争寻求达成协议。

"在某个时候我说，比比，我们必须停止这一切。我们必须停止它，"特朗普说，用昵称称呼内塔尼亚胡。

内塔尼亚胡在接受CNBC采访时表示，他与特朗普有时有"战术分歧"，但在伊朗相关的主要问题上他们意见一致。

（路透社各分社报道；安迪·沙利文、克拉伦斯·费尔南德斯、艾丹·刘易斯、加雷思·琼斯和蒂姆·里德撰写；林肯·费斯特、罗丝·拉塞尔和迪帕·巴宾顿编辑）
articles.summary:
LOADINGERROR LOADING
&

DUBAI, June 3 (Reuters) - Israel and Lebanon agreed to implement a ceasefire to end hostilities, the Trump administration said on Wednesday, in a boost to hopes for a broader deal to end the U.S.-Israeli war on Iran.

Tehran, which had conditioned any deal with the U.S. in part on an end to fighting between Israel and Lebanon, earlier struck Kuwait, damaging its airport and injuring dozens while the U.S. military carried out strikes near the Strait of Hormuz.

Advertisement

The Israel-Lebanon ceasefire is contingent on a complete cessation of fire from the Iran-aligned Hezbollah militia and the evacuation of all its operatives from the South Litani Sector, a joint statement released by the U.S. State Department said following negotiations in Washington.

The two sides had agreed last month to a ceasefire but hostilities had continued. Israel invaded Lebanon in March in pursuit of Lebanese militant group Hezbollah, which fired across the border in support of Tehran.

Advertisement

The attacks on Kuwait and in the strait are the latest to test a shaky ceasefire between the U.S., Israel and Iran, sending oil prices up nearly 2%, as the strait remains largely closed more than three months after the U.S. and Israel launched strikes on Iran.

Flights at Kuwait International Airport were suspended after an Iranian drone and missile attack damaged airport facilities and diplomatic missions, killing one person and injuring more than 60 others, Kuwaiti authorities and state media said.

Kuwait Airways and Jazeera Airways later resumed flights after taking safety measures, the civil aviation authority said.

Advertisement

Iran’s elite Revolutionary Guards said they did not fire at Kuwait’s airport and blamed the destruction on U.S. interceptor missiles that failed to hit their targets, according to Iranian state media.

The U.S. military said that was not accurate, and that Iranian drones targeted the airport deliberately.

Earlier, Iranian media reported the Revolutionary Guards had attacked the headquarters of the U.S. Fifth Fleet in Bahrain and a U.S. airbase, as well as a vessel identified as Panaya. U.S. Central Command denied its bases had been hit and said Iranian ballistic missiles failed to strike their targets in the region.

Advertisement

CENTCOM said it had carried out a new round of “defensive strikes” in southern Iran, targeted missile launch sites and Iranian boats seeking to lay mines, and conducted strikes on Qeshm Island near the Strait of Hormuz after attempted Iranian attacks.

CEASEFIRE STRAINED BY FLARE-UPS

Since the U.S. and Israeli strikes on Iran began on February 28, Tehran has repeatedly attacked targets in the Gulf region, home to U.S. military bases.

Advertisement

Hostilities have periodically flared up in recent weeks despite a ceasefire agreed in early April, as the U.S. has pushed to reopen the Strait of Hormuz, which handled roughly a fifth of global oil and liquefied natural gas shipments before the war.

Last week, Iran and the U.S. signalled progress towards a tentative initial agreement to halt the war and reopen the strait, but the two sides have yet to sign off on the deal, which would leave more complex negotiations for later.

Iran’s Foreign Minister Abbas Araqchi told Lebanese broadcaster Al Mayadeen on Wednesday that talks had not been cut off but no progress had been made.

Advertisement

In addition to Tehran conditioning a deal on an end to fighting in Lebanon, it also wants access to billions of dollars in oil revenue, waivers on sanctions on crude exports, a lifting of a U.S. blockade on its ports and continued leverage over the strait.

U.S. President Donald Trump, who is under pressure to bring down fuel prices, has said his top priority is to stop Iran from acquiring nuclear weapons. Iran says its atomic program is for peaceful purposes. In a podcast interview released on Wednesday, Trump said Iran had agreed to not have a nuclear weapon and that Khamenei was involved in negotiations.

Later on Wednesday Trump suggested there could be progress in negotiations with Iran as soon as this weekend.

Advertisement

“If it happens, it could happen over the weekend,” Trump told reporters in the White House’s Oval Office, without elaborating on what he expected to happen within that timeframe.

Trump said that parties were working to separate the issue of reopening the strait from the conflict in Lebanon.

Advertisement

ISRAEL KEEPS UP STRIKES ON LEBANON

The war has killed thousands, mainly in Iran and Lebanon, while causing global economic pain by severely disrupting energy supplies and other shipping.

It also sparked the latest round of conflict between Israel and Hezbollah.

On Wednesday, Israeli drone strikes killed at least six people in southern Lebanon and targeted a car just south of Beirut, Lebanese security sources said, while Israel said it intercepted a hostile aircraft likely fired by Hezbollah.

Araqchi said Iran would respond decisively if Israel attacks Beirut.

In his podcast comments, Trump acknowledged having called Israel’s Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu “crazy” during a reportedly expletive-filled phone exchange over the fighting in Lebanon as he sought a deal over the wider war.

Advertisement

“At some point I said, Bibi, we got to stop this. We got to stop it,” Trump said, referring to Netanyahu by his nickname.

Netanyahu told CNBC in an interview that he and Trump sometimes had “tactical disagreements” but that they agreed on the main issues concerning Iran.

(Reporting by Reuters bureaus; Writing by Andy Sullivan, Clarence Fernandez, Aidan Lewis, Gareth Jones and Tim Reid; Editing by Lincoln Feast, Ros Russell and Deepa Babington)

Advertisement

articles.title: UK Lawmaker Sues Elon Musk's xAI For Creating Fake Sexualized Images Of Her
articles.content:
伦敦6月3日电（路透社）——英国议员杰西·阿萨托正在对埃隆·马斯克旗下的xAI公司提起诉讼，她周三在一份声明中表示，Grok人工智能平台被用来制作她的虚假色情图像。

Grok通过马斯克的社交媒体平台X进行分发，由于今年早些时候有指控称该平台被用于制作非自愿的色情图像，目前正面临多个国家的监管调查。

“Grok制造了深度伪造的色情内容和性暗示内容，这些内容伤害了成千上万的女性和儿童，”属于首相基尔·斯塔默工党阵营的阿萨托在声明中表示。

“这种能力并非偶然，也不是误用，而是其开发人员的有意设计。我提起这起案件，是为了追究这些设计选择的责任。”

xAI没有立即回应置评请求。

xAI是马斯克旗下火箭和太空探索公司SpaceX的一部分，该公司预计将在本月晚些时候进行可能是史上最大规模的首次公开募股。

阿萨托办公室在声明中表示，在她1月谴责Grok之后，有用户制作并分享了描绘她穿着比基尼的虚假图片，以及一段显示她“被下药并准备遭受性侵”的视频。

3月，巴尔的摩市起诉了xAI，声称Grok生成虚假色情图像的能力违反了该市的消费者保护法。

1月中旬，xAI表示已限制Grok中的图像编辑功能，并在“非法的司法管辖区”阻止用户生成穿着暴露服装的人员图像。

2月初，路透社发现，即使在实施新限制后，Grok仍然继续生成人物的性暗示图像，即使用户明确警告这些对象并未同意。

代表阿萨托的律师事务所AWO表示，阿萨托已在英国高等法院提出索赔，指控违反数据保护法和滥用其私人信息。她正在寻求包括损害赔偿、正式承认所发生的事情是非法的，以及要求xAI停止所有进一步违法行为的补救措施。

“这是首起测试人工智能系统设计责任的案件之一，我们希望这能向人工智能开发者明确表示，安全不能是事后的附加考虑，”AWO的法律总监拉维·奈克表示。

（威廉·詹姆斯报道；辛西娅·奥斯特曼编辑）
articles.summary:
LOADINGERROR LOADING
&

LONDON, June 3 (Reuters) - British lawmaker Jess Asato is suing Elon Musk’s xAI, saying in a statement on Wednesday the Grok AI platform had been used to create fake sexualized images of her.

Grok, distributed through Musk’s social media platform X, is currently subject to regulatory probes in several countries after an outcry earlier this year over its use to create non-consensual sexualized images.

Advertisement

“Grok created deepfake pornography and sexualized content which harmed thousands of women and children,” Asato, who is a member of Prime Minister Keir Starmer’s Labour Party, said in a statement.

“Its ability is not an accident, nor misuse, it is a design choice by its creators. In launching this case, I am pursuing accountability for those choices.”

xAI did not immediately respond to a request for comment.

In mid-January xAI said it restricted image editing in Grok, and blocked users from generating images of people in revealing clothing in “jurisdictions where it’s illegal.”

Advertisement

In early February, Reuters found that even after new curbs, Grok continued to generate sexualized images of people even when users explicitly warned that the subjects do not consent.

xAI is part of Musk’s rocket and space exploration company SpaceX, which is expected to launch what could become the largest IPO in history later this month.

Advertisement

The statement from Asato’s office said that after she condemned Grok in January, users created and shared fake images depicting her in a bikini and a video showing her “being chloroformed and prepared for a sexual assault.”

In March, the City of Baltimore sued xAI, claiming the Grok’s ability to create fake sexualized images violated the city’s consumer protection law.

Law firm AWO said Asato had filed a claim at the High Court in England for breaches of data protection law and misuse of her private information. She is seeking remedies including damages, a formal acknowledgement that what happened to her was illegal and an order requiring xAI to stop all further illegality.

Advertisement

“This is one of the first claims to test liability for the design of an AI system, and we hope it will make it clear to AI developers that safety cannot be an afterthought,” said Ravi Naik, legal director of AWO.

(Reporting by William James; Editing by Cynthia Osterman)

Related

elon musk

spacex

grok

x.ai

articles.title: Ukrainian Drones Hit St Petersburg Oil Terminal Ahead Of Russian Economic Forum
articles.content:
乌克兰远程无人机周三袭击了圣彼得堡一座石油终端并将其点燃，乌克兰总统泽连斯基表示。浓烟滚滚笼罩着这座普京出生的城市，此时该市正在举办俄罗斯吸引外资的顶级经济论坛。

泽连斯基在社交媒体上称，这些无人机飞行超过1000公里（600英里）袭击了俄罗斯第二大城市的目标。前一天，莫斯科对基辅和其他乌克兰城市发动了大规模无人机和导弹袭击。

俄罗斯当局仅表示乌方袭击了圣彼得堡基础设施，未提供更多细节。机场短暂停飞，手机网络被切断。

前线因无人机群阻碍推进几乎没有移动，双方都寻求通过远程打击获得优势。俄罗斯全面入侵邻国的战争已持续四年多，看不到结束迹象。

最新袭击再次让普京难堪。数周前，他因担心乌方无人机袭击而缩减了莫斯科胜利日阅兵规模。

普京周五将在圣彼得堡经济论坛上发言，克里姆林宫将此视为重要活动。该论坛有时被称为俄罗斯达沃斯。

自2022年2月24日俄罗斯发动对乌克兰全面战争以来，主要西方投资者和官员一直回避参与。沙特阿拉伯今年作为特邀嘉宾出席，预计派出大型商业代表团。

泽连斯基表示，乌克兰仅以与俄战争努力相关的“合法目标”为目标，并暗示基辅计划升级远程无人机攻击。他对媒体称：“我们能够增加大规模打击规模的只是时间问题。”

圣彼得堡的袭击发生在俄对乌攻击造成23名平民死亡、151人受伤的第二天。莫斯科履行了升级攻势的威胁。

克里姆林宫发言人佩斯科夫周三表示，俄罗斯的深度打击已具有“系统性”特征。

乌克兰的远程攻击旨在削弱俄罗斯的石油生产——这是莫斯科的主要资金来源，并干扰武器生产。基辅多次以圣彼得堡和附近港口的石油设施为目标。

泽连斯基对本国政府官员表示失望，称购买爱国者系统的协议“已在最高政治层面”达成，但实施因财务、法律和技术考虑而受阻。他在社交媒体上表示：“等待时间太长了。”他要求官员解除购买障碍，否则将做出“重大人事决定”。

正在基辅访问的北约秘书长吕特周三表示，美国向乌克兰提供的拦截导弹仍在继续。他在新闻发布会上称，美国正在“尽其所能”继续供应，尽管受限于生产速度。吕特还表示，年轻的俄罗斯人及其家庭被莫斯科“坑了”，因为加入俄军意味着糟糕的训练和装备，以及战场上受伤后很低的存活机会。

据乌克兰无人系统部队指挥官布罗夫迪称，乌克兰其他无人机夜间袭击使俄罗斯导弹护卫舰“博基伊”号起火，该舰当时正在喀琅施塔得海军基地的干船坞中。喀琅施塔得是俄罗斯波罗的海舰队位于圣彼得堡以西的一个古老基地。

泽连斯基表示，无人机还袭击了俄罗斯位于坦波夫地区的一家武器生产制造厂，该地区距乌克兰600公里（370英里）。

俄罗斯国防部表示，防空部队一夜之间击落了354架乌克兰无人机。

据克里姆林宫任命的顿涅茨克领导人普希林称，在俄罗斯控制的乌克兰顿涅茨克地区，乌克兰袭击击中了一辆从莫斯科驶往克里米亚半岛的公交车，造成7人死亡，11人受伤。

据该地区州长阿诺金称，在斯摩棱斯克地区，两名消防员在乌克兰无人机袭击中丧生，另有两名消防员和一名当地居民受伤。

与此同时，据乌克兰空军称，俄罗斯昨晚向乌克兰发射了198架远程无人机，防空系统击落了189架。

乌克兰北部苏梅地区当局表示，在过去24小时内，俄罗斯袭击造成1名平民死亡，15人受伤，其中包括3名儿童。

据地区当局称，在南部赫尔松，俄罗斯夜间炮击和无人机袭击造成一名86岁妇女死亡，另有5人受伤。
articles.summary:
LOADINGERROR LOADING
&

Ukrainian long-range drones struck an oil terminal in St. Petersburg and set it ablaze, President Volodymyr Zelenskyy said Wednesday, sending smoke billowing over the city where President Vladimir Putin was born as it hosts Russia’s leading event for attracting foreign capital.

The drones flew more than 1,000 kilometers (600 miles) to hit the terminal in Russia’s second-largest city, Zelenskyy said on social media, a day after Moscow launched a major drone and missile attack on Kyiv and other Ukrainian cities.

Advertisement

Russian authorities said only that the Ukrainian drone strike targeted St. Petersburg’s infrastructure, without providing details. The city’s airport briefly suspended flights overnight because of the attack. Authorities cut off mobile internet services.

With the front line barely moving as swarms of drones hinder advances, both sides have sought an edge by launching long-range strikes. The war that followed Russia’s full-scale invasion of its neighbor is more than four years old, with no end in sight.

Advertisement

Ukraine’s drone successes embarrass Putin

The latest strikes are another embarrassment for Putin, weeks after he pruned back an annual Victory Day parade in Moscow because of fears of Ukrainian drone attacks.

Putin is set to speak on Friday at the economic forum in St. Petersburg that the Kremlin views as a prestige event. The gathering is sometimes called Russia’s Davos, likening it to the World Economic Forum held in Switzerland.

Major Western investors and officials have stayed away since Russia launched its all-out war against Ukraine on Feb. 24, 2022. Saudi Arabia is a special guest this year and is due to send a large business delegation.

Advertisement

Zelenskyy said Ukraine aimed only at “legitimate targets” related to Russia’s war effort and indicated that Kyiv plans to escalate its long-range drone attacks. “It is only a matter of time when we will be able to increase the scale of our own mass strikes,” he told reporters.

The strikes on St. Petersburg came a day after Russia’s attack on Ukraine killed 23 civilians and wounded 151 other people, as Moscow followed through with its threat of escalating its barrages.

Kremlin spokesman Dmitry Peskov said Wednesday that Russia’s deep strikes have already taken on a “systematic” character.

Advertisement

Ukraine needs more air defense help

Ukraine’s own long-range attacks are aimed at diminishing Russia’s oil production, which is a key source of funding for Moscow, and disrupting weapon production. Kyiv has repeatedly targeted oil facilities in St. Petersburg and nearby ports.

But Ukraine is short of American-made Patriot air defense missiles, in part because of U.S. stocks being depleted by the Iran war, leaving it vulnerable to Russia’s ballistic missiles.

Zelenskyy on Wednesday expressed frustration with his own government’s officials, saying there’s an agreement “at the highest political level” for the purchase of Patriot systems, but implementation is being held up by financial, legal and technical considerations.

Advertisement

“The wait has taken too long,” he said on social media, demanding that officials unblock the purchase or there will be “serious personnel decisions.”

NATO Secretary-General Mark Rutte, visiting Kyiv on Wednesday for talks with Ukrainian officials, said the flow of interceptor missiles from the U.S. to Ukraine continues. The U.S. is “doing what it can” to keep supplying them although it is limited by the production rate, he told a news conference.

Rutte also said young Russians and their families “are being sold a raw deal” by Moscow, as incorporation in the Russian military dooms soldiers to poor training and equipment and low chances of surviving battlefield wounds.

Advertisement

Ukraine also strikes a Russian naval base

Other Ukrainian drone attacks overnight set fire to the Russian guided-missile corvette Boikiy, which was in dry dock at the Kronstadt naval base, according to Robert Brovdi, commander of Ukraine’s Unmanned Systems Forces. Kronstadt is an old base for Russia’s Baltic Fleet located west of St. Petersburg.

Drones also hit a Russian manufacturing plant involved in weapon production in the Tambov region, 600 kilometers (370 miles) from Ukraine, Zelenskyy said.

Advertisement

Russia’s Defense Ministry said air defenses downed 354 Ukrainian drones overnight.

In the Russia-controlled part of Ukraine’s Donetsk region, a Ukrainian strike hit a bus that was traveling from Moscow to the Crimean Peninsula, killing seven people and wounding 11 others, according to the Kremlin-appointed head of Donetsk, Denis Pushilin.

In the Smolensk region, two firefighters were killed by a Ukrainian drone attack, according to the regional governor, Vasily Anokhin. He said that two other firefighters and a local resident were wounded.

Meanwhile, Russia fired 198 long-range drones at Ukraine last night, according to Ukraine’s air force, with air defenses neutralizing 189.

Advertisement

Authorities in Ukraine’s northern Sumy region said that over the previous 24 hours, one civilian was killed and 15 more were wounded, including three children, by Russian strikes.

In the southern Kherson, Russian overnight shelling and drone strikes killed an 86-year-old woman and wounded five other people, according to regional authorities.
